name: Circular route 17 — São Brás de Alportel (Serra do Caldeirão)
name_original: Percurso circular 17 — São Brás de Alportel (Serra do Caldeirão)
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around São Brás de Alportel traverse a diverse landscape, situated between the uplands and the sea in the Eastern Algarve. The region features lush green hills, extensive olive groves, and significant cork oak forests, providing varied terrain for cyclists. The Barrocal region, a fertile limestone area, is abundant with olive, carob, fig, and almond trees, while the São Brás mountains offer scenic ascents and descents. This area provides a network of routes suitable for exploring its natural features and cultural heritage. Best touring cycling routes around São Brás De Alportel The most popular touring cycling route is Circular route 17 — São Brás de Alportel (Serra do Caldeirão), a 32.1 miles (51.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain through the Serra do Caldeirão, showcasing the region's hilly interior. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Scenic Road for Cycling – Road to Cachopo loop from São Brás de Alportel, a difficult 42.1 miles (67.7 km) path. This route provides extensive views and challenging climbs through the scenic countryside towards Cachopo. Local touring cyclists also love the Loulé Market Hall – Loulé Market loop from São Brás de Alportel, a 19.5 miles (31.3 km) trail leading through the Barrocal region, often completed in about 2 hours 3 minutes. Touring cycling around São Brás De Alportel is defined by its cork oak forests, olive groves, and the rolling hills of the Serra do Caldeirão. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in São Brás De Alportel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 36 reviews. More than 323 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore São Brás De Alportel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes will I encounter on touring cycling routes around São Brás de Alportel?
A: The routes in São Brás de Alportel offer a diverse landscape, transitioning from lush green hills and extensive olive groves to significant cork oak forests. You'll also traverse the fertile Barrocal region, abundant with olive, carob, fig, and almond trees, and experience the rolling hills of the Serra do Caldeirão.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for different fitness levels?
A: Yes, São Brás de Alportel offers a variety of touring cycling routes catering to different abilities. Out of 44 available tours, you'll find 6 easy, 20 moderate, and 18 difficult routes, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in São Brás de Alportel?
A: The touring cycling routes around São Brás de Alportel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 36 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the cork oak forests, and the charming olive groves.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2283140790/circular-route-17-sao-bras-de-alportel-serra-do-caldeirao" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular route 17 — São Brás de Alportel (Serra do Caldeirão)&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 51.6 km with significant elevation gain through the Serra do Caldeirão.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes with extensive views?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge and expansive views, consider the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10291335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Road for Cycling – Road to Cachopo loop from São Brás de Alportel&lt;/a&gt;. This 67.7 km route features challenging climbs and offers extensive vistas through the scenic countryside towards Cachopo.

Q: What cultural or historical attractions can I explore while cycling?
A: São Brás de Alportel has a rich history tied to cork production. While cycling, you can learn about this heritage by visiting the Alportel Museum or exploring the 'Cork Route' which highlights traditional harvesting. The town center itself offers charming traditional architecture and cobbled streets.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through local towns or markets?
A: Yes, you can cycle routes that connect to nearby towns and offer a glimpse into local life. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31105467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loulé Market Hall – Loulé Market loop from São Brás de Alportel&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 31.3 km trail that leads through the Barrocal region to Loulé, perfect for exploring its market.

Q: What kind of natural features or viewpoints can I expect to see?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to see extensive cork oak forests, olive groves, and the unique flora of the Barrocal region. Scenic viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4099481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroteia Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4037493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alcaria do Cume and the Atlantic Ocean&lt;/a&gt; offer expansive vistas, sometimes extending to the ocean.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Serra do Caldeirão mountains?
A: Yes, the Serra do Caldeirão is a prominent feature of the region's cycling landscape. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2283140790/circular-route-17-sao-bras-de-alportel-serra-do-caldeirao" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular route 17 — São Brás de Alportel (Serra do Caldeirão)&lt;/a&gt; take you directly into these mountains, offering significant elevation and showcasing the hilly interior.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in São Brás de Alportel?
A: São Brás de Alportel benefits from a mild climate, making touring cycling enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, with less intense heat than summer and typically dry conditions.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by unique local landmarks or points of interest?
A: Many routes offer glimpses of local life and unique spots. For instance, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17218844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CamperDream Ecopark – Filipe Spring loop from São Brás de Alportel&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the CamperDream Ecopark. You might also encounter traditional villages and agricultural landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the Barrocal region's unique flora?
A: Yes, the Barrocal region, situated between the mountains and the sea, is characterized by its fertile limestone soil and abundant trees like olive, carob, fig, and almond. Many routes, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32177801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pig Farm in Barrocal – Filipe Spring loop from São Brás de Alportel&lt;/a&gt;, traverse this area, allowing you to experience its unique agricultural landscape.
tips: not too difficult climb to Botelho where you have a nice view of Sao Bras de Alportel and surroundings. the last part to the top you have to walk a bit ....

From this viewpoint, the horizon stretches across almost the entire municipality. It's the ideal place to take a break (there's a picnic area waiting for you) before cycling again through endless landscapes.

waypoints.name: View of São Brás de Alportel from Botelho
